User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the UnMac service. Assumes an external authentication system for credentials, so no sensitive authentication data is stored here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Email address of the user, used for identification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's record was created.

UnblockRequest.title: UnblockRequest
UnblockRequest.description: Details of a user's request to unblock a MAC address, including device and network information, and plan selection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UnblockRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this unblock request. (Relationship: User 1:N UnblockRequest)

macAddress.description: The MAC address provided by the user for unblocking.

deviceName.description: The name of the device associated with the MAC address, provided by the user.

wifiProvider.description: The WiFi service provider selected by the user (e.g., 'Jio', 'Airtel', 'BSNL').

wifiName.description: The SSID (Service Set Identifier) of the WiFi network.

requestDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the unblock request was submitted.

status.description: Current status of the unblock request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Unblocked', 'Failed', 'Cancelled').

planType.description: The type of plan selected for this request ('Trial' or 'Paid').

trialStartTime.description: The start timestamp for a free trial unblock request. Applicable only when 'planType' is 'Trial'.

trialEndTime.description: The end timestamp for a free trial unblock request. Applicable only when 'planType' is 'Trial'.

subscriptionId.description: Reference to the active paid subscription that enables this unblock request. Applicable only when 'planType' is 'Paid'. (Relationship: Subscription 1:N UnblockRequest)

unblockedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the MAC address was successfully unblocked.

troubleshootingNotes.description: Notes or suggestions generated by the Smart Troubleshooting Assistant or backend related to this request.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Represents a user's paid subscription to the UnMac service, granting access to premium features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this subscription. (Relationship: User 1:N Subscription)

startDate.description: The date when the subscription became active.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is set to expire.

isActive.description: A boolean indicating whether the subscription is currently active.

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference ID from the external payment gateway for the transaction associated with this subscription.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the subscription record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update made to the subscription record.

ContactInquiry.title: ContactInquiry
ContactInquiry.description: Stores inquiries submitted by users through the website's contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactInquiry entity.

name.description: The name of the person submitting the inquiry.

email.description: The email address of the inquirer, for response purposes.

subject.description: The subject line or topic of the inquiry.

message.description: The full message or content of the inquiry.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the inquiry was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible only by the owning user. This path-based ownership is fundamental for secure authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Stores details of a user's MAC address unblock requests. This subcollection structure ensures that requests are owned by and accessible only to the user specified in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' in path for authorization independence, making the document's internal 'userId' field not strictly necessary for ownership checks in rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who made the unblock request, matching request.auth.uid.

params.name: unblockRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the unblock request.

definition.description: Stores a user's paid subscriptions. This subcollection structure ensures that subscriptions are owned by and accessible only to the user specified in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' in path for authorization independence, making the document's internal 'userId' field not strictly necessary for ownership checks in rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the subscription, matching request.auth.uid.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique ID of the subscription.

definition.description: Stores inquiries submitted through the contact form. Designed for public creation (anonymous users) and restricted read/update access (e.g., to admin roles). This is a top-level collection as it's not strictly user-owned, ensuring structural segregation.

params.name: inquiryId
params.description: The unique ID of the contact inquiry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for UnMac prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The chosen structure leverages path-based ownership and structural segregation to achieve authorization independence and support query-aware rules (QAPs).

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
The primary strategy for achieving Authorization Independence is through **path-based ownership and structural segregation**. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore security rules, which are critical for robust atomic operations (transactions/batches) and simplified debugging.
*   **For User-owned data (`UnblockRequest`, `Subscription`):** These entities are structured as subcollections directly under the `users` collection: `/users/{userId}/unblockRequests/{unblockRequestId}` and `/users/{userId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}`. The `userId` is an explicit part of the document's path. In Firestore security rules, `request.auth.uid` can be directly compared against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path (e.g., `match /users/{userId}/... { allow read, write: if userId == request.auth.uid; }`). This design inherently denormalizes the authorization context to the path itself, eliminating the need to read the `userId` field from the document's content for authorization checks. The `userId` field still exists within the `UnblockRequest` and `Subscription` documents for data integrity and querying purposes, but it is not the *primary* source of authorization for ownership-based rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   **Path-Based Ownership for Secure Lists:** By structuring `UnblockRequest` and `Subscription` under `/users/{userId}/...`, `list` operations are inherently secure and do not require rules to act as filters. When a user requests to `list` documents in `/users/{myUserId}/unblockRequests`, Firestore security rules evaluate the request against the path segment `{myUserId}`. If `myUserId` matches `request.auth.uid`, the rule grants access. If a malicious user attempts to list documents in `/users/{otherUserId}/unblockRequests`, the rule `userId == request.auth.uid` will fail, preventing access before any data is returned. This ensures that the database only returns documents the user is authorized to see, preventing client-side filtering vulnerabilities.
*   **Structural Segregation for `ContactInquiry`:** The `ContactInquiry` collection is a top-level collection (`/contactInquiries/{inquiryId}`). This segregation is crucial because these inquiries are not 'user-owned' in the same way `UnblockRequest` or `Subscription` are. They are a global collection for support. This separation ensures that a simple `list` rule for `/contactInquiries` for administrators does not accidentally expose sensitive inquiries to regular users, nor does it require complex filtering logic within rules. It enables secure public creation and restricted management access.

This structure provides a robust foundation for secure and maintainable Firestore security rules for the UnMac application by ensuring authorization logic is explicit, efficient, and deeply integrated into the data's hierarchical organization.
